text:
Part 7 - OCR on a Scanned Image

Create src/ocr/ocr_utils.py and start with a single scanned image OCR is needed when text is
trapped inside pixels, for example in scans, photographed forms, or image-only files.

Download a test image from the internet or create your own (e.g., a screenshot of some text, a
scanned document, etc.). Save it as data/raw/images/test_scan.png. You can also use images we
took from api in week2 if they contain text. To apply OCR to images pytesseract should be
used. Preprocessing significantly improves OCR accuracy compared to raw extraction.

text:
Part 7 - OCR on a Scanned Image

Create src/ocr/ocr_utils.py and start with a single scanned image OCR is needed when text is
trapped inside pixels, for example in scans, photographed forms, or image-only files.

Download a test image from the internet or create your own (e.g., a screenshot of some text, a
scanned document, etc.). Save it as data/raw/images/test_scan.png. You can also use images we
took from api in week2 if they contain text. To apply OCR to images pytesseract should be
used. Preprocessing significantly improves OCR accuracy compared to raw extraction.

Create src/ocr/ocr_utils.py and start with a single scanned image OCR is needed when text is
trapped inside pixels, for example in scans, photographed forms, or image-only files.

Download a test image from the internet or create your own (e.g., a screenshot of some text, a
scanned document, etc.). Save it as data/raw/images/test_scan.png. You can also use images we
took from api in week2 if they contain text. To apply OCR to images pytesseract should be
used. Preprocessing significantly improves OCR accuracy compared to raw extraction.

text:
Part 7 - OCR on a Scanned Image

Create src/ocr/ocr_utils.py and start with a single scanned image OCR is needed when text is
trapped inside pixels, for example in scans, photographed forms, or image-only files.

Download a test image from the internet or create your own (e.g., a screenshot of some text, a
scanned document, etc.). Save it as data/raw/images/test_scan.png. You can also use images we
took from api in week2 if they contain text. To apply OCR to images pytesseract should be
used. Preprocessing significantly improves OCR accuracy compared to raw extraction.

text:
Part 7 - OCR on a Scanned Image

Create src/ocr/ocr_utils.py and start with a single scanned image OCR is needed when text is
trapped inside pixels, for example in scans, photographed forms, or image-only files.

Download a test image from the internet or create your own (e.g., a screenshot of some text, a
scanned document, etc.). Save it as data/raw/images/test_scan.png. You can also use images we
took from api in week2 if they contain text. To apply OCR to images pytesseract should be
used. Preprocessing significantly improves OCR accuracy compared to raw extraction.

text:
Part 7 - OCR on a Scanned Image

Create src/ocr/ocr_utils.py and start with a single scanned image OCR is needed when text is
trapped inside pixels, for example in scans, photographed forms, or image-only files.

Download a test image from the internet or create your own (e.g., a screenshot of some text, a
scanned document, etc.). Save it as data/raw/images/test_scan.png. You can also use images we
took from api in week2 if they contain text. To apply OCR to images pytesseract should be
used. Preprocessing significantly improves OCR accuracy compared to raw extraction.
